behaviors.description: Cron parser rejects invalid expressions (month 13, day 32, malformed syntax) with descriptive errors.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser-local validation and error construction. Behavior text is about one component rejecting bad input, not cross-module handoff. Rejected higher level: no behavior text requiring parser->scheduler interaction, OS/network/subprocess, or model output.
behaviors.test_description: Call the parser directly with a fixed invalid-case canary set (`13` month, `32` day, malformed token counts/chars) and assert structured error type plus message content identifying the invalid field/value.

behaviors.description: Next-run-time calculator returns correct timestamps for standard cron expressions, including last-day-of-month and nearest-weekday edge cases.
behaviors.justification: Real failure often appears at parser->schedule-object->calculator handoff for special semantics (`L`, nearest weekday). Unit-testing calculator internals alone can miss representation mismatches. Rejected higher levels: no required real OS/network/subprocess; no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Use end-to-end in-process wiring from cron string input through parser and next-N calculator, with fixed start times and expected timestamp sequences for normal and hard-edge expressions (`last day`, `nearest weekday`).

behaviors.description: Overlap detector finds schedule pairs firing within minimum gap and reports conflicting windows.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets interaction between schedule generation and overlap logic (time-window derivation + gap policy), a module-boundary failure mode. Rejected System: no behavior text requiring real OS primitives/subprocess/network. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser + next-run generator + overlap detector in-process; generate windows for two cron schedules under a configured gap (e.g., 5 min), assert detected conflict intervals and non-conflict controls.

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling handles DST spring-forward gap and fall-back ambiguity for a 2:30 AM job.
behaviors.justification: Needs real timezone-rule application across scheduler components, but can be caught with in-process timezone library + real zone data; behavior text does not require subprocess, OS eventing, or network. Rejected System specifically because wording only requires correct timestamp computation across DST, not OS-level clock/process behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run parser + timezone-aware next-run computation in-process using a real IANA zone with known DST transitions; assert spring-forward handling (nonexistent 2:30) and fall-back handling (ambiguous 2:30) follow defined policy.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: If implementation relies on OS tzdata outside process control, promote to System in that environment.

behaviors.description: When computed next run is in the past after downtime, engine applies missed-run policy (skip vs backfill).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is policy engine + schedule computation + persisted/derived timing state handoff. A single-function unit test can miss policy orchestration bugs. Rejected System: downtime is modeled by timestamps/state, not required real OS outage/subprocess. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: In-process test with scheduler components wired together, seeded last-run/now timestamps simulating downtime, and two policy configs (`skip`, `backfill`); assert chosen execution decision and produced next run(s).
